result:
vfs_read is a real function too. All four call sites confirmed as real functions (none are macros). Summary:

## `ksys_read()` — `fs/read_write.c:602-619`

Functions called directly, in source order:

| Line | Call | Nature |
|------|------|--------|
| 604 | `fdget_pos(fd)` | `static inline` function, `include/linux/file.h:72` — wraps `__fdget_pos()` and `__to_fd()` (not a macro) |
| 608 | `file_ppos(f.file)` | `static inline` function, defined just above in the same file at `fs/read_write.c:597` — returns `&file->f_pos` or `NULL` if `FMODE_STREAM` |
| 613 | `vfs_read(f.file, buf, count, ppos)` | ordinary extern function, defined `fs/read_write.c:450`, declared `include/linux/fs.h:1964` |
| 616 | `fdput_pos(f)` | `static inline` function, `include/linux/file.h:77` — internally calls `__f_unlock_pos()` (conditionally) and `fdput()` |

Note: none of the four are function-like macros — I checked and all resolve to `static inline` functions or plain extern functions. `fdget_pos`/`fdput_pos` internally wrap `__fdget_pos`/`__f_unlock_pos`/`fdput`, but those are nested calls inside those inline functions, not calls made directly by `ksys_read()` itself.
